title: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil

seo.title: Authentic Focaccia Genovese with Rosemary & Olive Oil
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Focaccia Genovese with our easy recipe. Infused with rosemary and olive oil, this Italian bread is perfect for any meal. Get pro tips for the best results!
longTailKeywords: Focaccia Genovese with rosemary and olive oil recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Focaccia Genovese with Rosemary & Olive Oil
h2Headings: How to Make Focaccia Genovese: Step-by-Step Instructions
seo.imageAlt: Golden Focaccia Genovese with fresh rosemary and olive oil, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a slice of authentic Focaccia Genovese, a classic Italian bread infused with fragrant rosemary and rich olive oil.

description: Immerse yourself in the rustic charm of Focaccia Genovese, a truly exceptional Italian flatbread. This recipe brings the authentic flavors of Genoa to your kitchen, featuring a crispy crust, a soft airy interior, and the aromatic embrace of fresh rosemary and golden olive oil. Perfect for sharing or savoring on your own, it is a taste of Ligurian sunshine.

ingredients.notes: plus more for drizzling and pan

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the warm water and yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy. This indicates the yeast is active and ready.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: Add the flour and fine sea salt to the yeast mixture. Mix with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. Gradually add 2 tablespoons of olive oil, incorporating it until the dough comes together. Transfer the dough to a lightly floured surface and knead for 5-7 minutes until smooth and elastic.

instructions.title: First Rise
instructions.text: Lightly oil a clean bowl with olive oil. Place the dough in the bowl, turning once to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Shape the Focaccia
instructions.text: Generously oil a 9x13 inch baking pan with olive oil. Gently transfer the risen dough to the prepared pan. Using your fingertips, gently stretch and press the dough to fill the pan, being careful not to deflate it too much. If the dough resists, let it rest for 10 minutes, then continue stretching.

instructions.title: Second Rise and Dimpling
instructions.text: Cover the pan with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let the focaccia rise for another 30-45 minutes in a warm place. Once risen, use your oiled fingertips to create deep dimples all over the surface of the dough. Drizzle generously with more extra virgin olive oil and sprinkle with chopped fresh rosemary and coarse sea salt.

instructions.title: Bake to Golden Perfection
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 220C (425F). Bake the focaccia for 25-35 minutes, or until it is golden brown and cooked through. The internal temperature should reach about 95C (200F). Remove from the oven and let it cool slightly in the pan before transferring to a wire rack. Drizzle with a final touch of olive oil if desired.

tips: For the best texture, use a high-quality extra virgin olive oil, both in the dough and for drizzling. It makes a significant difference in flavor.
Do not skip the dimpling step! It creates the characteristic pockets that hold the olive oil and makes the focaccia incredibly moist and flavorful.
Focaccia is best enjoyed fresh on the day it is baked. If you have leftovers, lightly toast them in the oven or a pan to revive their crispness.
article:
The aroma of freshly baked bread, earthy rosemary, and rich olive oil is a symphony that transports you straight to the sun-drenched Ligurian coast. Few Italian bakes capture this essence quite like Focaccia Genovese. This humble yet exquisite flatbread, a cornerstone of Italian baking, is renowned for its irresistibly crisp bottom, airy, tender interior, and a beautifully dimpled surface glistening with golden extra virgin olive oil and fragrant rosemary. It’s more than just bread; it's a culinary embrace, a testament to the power of simple, high-quality ingredients transformed into something truly magical.

## What is Focaccia Genovese?

Focaccia Genovese is the quintessential focaccia from Genoa, the capital city of the Liguria region in northwest Italy. Unlike its thicker, often more bread-like counterparts found in other parts of Italy, authentic Focaccia Genovese is characterized by its relatively thin profile, an incredibly soft and airy crumb, and a distinctively oily, almost fried-like crust. The magic lies in the generous use of extra virgin olive oil, both within the dough and liberally drizzled over the top before baking. This olive oil creates a fantastic contrast: a chewy, moist inside and a delightfully crisp exterior. The dimples, a signature feature, aren't just for aesthetics; they capture pools of that precious olive oil and coarse sea salt, ensuring every bite bursts with flavor. Historically, it was a practical bread for sailors and workers, easy to carry and satisfying. Today, it remains a beloved staple, embodying the soul of Ligurian cuisine with its emphasis on fresh, local ingredients, particularly the region's famed olive oil and aromatic herbs like rosemary.

## Ingredients for Authentic Focaccia Genovese

Crafting the perfect Focaccia Genovese begins with selecting the right ingredients. Quality truly makes a difference here, especially when it comes to the olive oil.

*   **Strong Bread Flour (Type 00 or All-Purpose):** A high-protein flour is essential for developing the gluten structure needed for that characteristic airy, chewy crumb. While Type 00 is traditional, a good quality all-purpose flour can also work well.
*   **Warm Water:** Activates the yeast and helps hydrate the flour. The temperature is crucial – not too hot to kill the yeast, not too cold to inhibit it.
*   **Active Dry Yeast:** The leavening agent that gives focaccia its lift and texture.
*   **Fine Sea Salt:** Enhances flavor and controls yeast activity.
*   **Sugar (optional, but recommended):** A pinch helps activate the yeast and contributes to a beautiful golden crust.
*   **Extra Virgin Olive Oil:** This is the star ingredient. Use a high-quality, fruity extra virgin olive oil. It’s incorporated into the dough, generously coats the baking pan, and is drizzled on top before baking. It’s responsible for the focaccia's signature flavor, texture, and golden hue.
*   **Fresh Rosemary:** Sprigs of fresh rosemary, roughly chopped, infuse the bread with their aromatic, piney notes. Dried rosemary can be used in a pinch, but fresh is vastly superior.
*   **Coarse Sea Salt (e.g., Maldon or Fleur de Sel):** Sprinkled over the dimpled surface, these larger salt crystals provide bursts of salty crunch and visual appeal.

## How to Make Focaccia Genovese: Step-by-Step Instructions

Making Focaccia Genovese is a rewarding process that requires a little patience but is surprisingly straightforward.

1.  **Activate the Yeast:** In a large bowl, combine the warm water, yeast, and sugar (if using). Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.
2.  **Mix the Dough:** Add the flour and fine sea salt to the yeast mixture. Drizzle in 2-3 tablespoons of extra virgin olive oil. Mix with a wooden spoon or a stand mixer with a dough hook until a shaggy dough forms.
3.  **Knead:** Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until it's smooth, elastic, and no longer sticky. Alternatively, use a stand mixer on medium speed for 5-7 minutes. The dough should be soft and pliable.
4.  **First Rise:** Lightly grease a clean bowl with olive oil. Place the dough in the bowl, turning it once to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp tea towel and let it rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until doubled in size.
5.  **Prepare the Pan:** Liberally grease a 9x13 inch (or similar size) baking pan with 3-4 tablespoons of extra virgin olive oil. Don't be shy; this creates the crispy bottom.
6.  **Shape the Dough:** Gently deflate the risen dough and transfer it to the prepared baking pan. Gently stretch and press the dough to fill the pan, being careful not to tear it. If it resists, let it rest for 10-15 minutes, then continue stretching.
7.  **Second Rise:** Once the dough fills the pan, cover it again and let it rise for another 30-45 minutes in a warm place. It should look puffy.
8.  **Dimple and Top:** Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Using your fingertips, gently but firmly press deep dimples all over the surface of the dough, taking care not to puncture it. Drizzle another 2-3 tablespoons of extra virgin olive oil generously over the entire surface, ensuring it pools in the dimples. Sprinkle evenly with fresh rosemary and coarse sea salt.
9.  **Bake:** Bake for 20-30 minutes, or until the Focaccia Genovese is golden brown on top and the bottom is crisp.
10. **Cool:** Carefully remove the focaccia from the pan and transfer it to a wire rack to cool slightly before slicing and serving.

## Tips for Perfect Focaccia Genovese

Achieving that perfect, airy Focaccia Genovese with its signature crust requires attention to a few key details:

*   **Embrace the Olive Oil:** This cannot be stressed enough. High-quality extra virgin olive oil is non-negotiable. Don't skimp on it, either in the dough, in the pan, or on top. It's the secret to the focaccia's flavor, texture, and golden finish.
*   **Hydration is Key:** Focaccia dough is relatively wet. This high hydration contributes to its airy, open crumb. Don't be tempted to add too much extra flour during kneading; a slightly sticky dough is normal.
*   **Long, Slow Fermentation:** While the recipe provides standard rising times, if you have the luxury, a longer, colder first rise (e.g., overnight in the refrigerator) can significantly deepen the flavor profile of your Focaccia Genovese. Just bring it to room temperature before shaping.
*   **Proper Kneading:** Adequate kneading develops the gluten, which is crucial for the focaccia's structure and chewiness. Don't cut this step short.
*   **Deep Dimples:** When creating the dimples, be assertive! You want them deep enough to hold those beautiful pools of olive oil and salt, which crisp up wonderfully during baking.
*   **Hot Oven:** A hot oven ensures a quick rise and a beautifully crisp crust. Ensure your oven is fully preheated.
*   **Fresh Rosemary:** While dried herbs can be convenient, fresh rosemary provides a much more vibrant and authentic flavor for your Focaccia Genovese.

## Serving Suggestions for Focaccia Genovese

The beauty of Focaccia Genovese lies in its versatility. It's delicious on its own but also makes a fantastic accompaniment to a wide range of dishes.

*   **Plain Snack:** There's nothing quite like a warm slice straight from the oven, perhaps with a little extra dip of olive oil.
*   **Antipasto Platter:** Serve it alongside cured meats, cheeses, marinated olives, and roasted vegetables for an authentic Italian spread.
*   **Soup or Salad Companion:** Its hearty yet light texture makes it perfect for dipping into a rich minestrone or serving alongside a fresh green salad.
*   **Sandwiches:** Slice it horizontally and fill it with your favorite Italian deli meats, cheeses, tomatoes, and pesto for an incredible sandwich.
*   **Breakfast Bread:** Surprisingly delicious with scrambled eggs or even just a smear of butter and jam for a savory-sweet start to the day.
*   **With Dips:** Perfect for dipping into balsamic vinegar and olive oil, hummus, or a creamy ricotta dip.

## Focaccia Genovese Variations & Substitutions

While the classic Focaccia Genovese with rosemary and olive oil is perfection, there are many ways to adapt and personalize this wonderful bread.

*   **Onion:** A classic Ligurian variation involves thinly sliced white or red onions, often caramelized slightly, scattered over the top before baking. This adds a wonderful sweetness and depth.
*   **Olives:** Pitted Kalamata or Ligurian Taggiasca olives pressed into the dough before baking are a popular addition, offering a briny counterpoint.
*   **Cherry Tomatoes:** Halved cherry tomatoes, pressed into the dough, release their juices during baking, creating pockets of sweet, tangy flavor.
*   **Cheese:** A sprinkle of grated Parmesan, Pecorino, or even dollops of soft Stracchino cheese can be added during the last few minutes of baking.
*   **Other Herbs:** While rosemary is traditional, you can experiment with fresh thyme, oregano, or a mix of Italian herbs.
*   **Gluten-Free:** While challenging to replicate the exact texture, a blend of gluten-free flours (e.g., rice, tapioca, potato starch) with xanthan gum can be used, though the hydration and kneading will need adjustment.
*   **Whole Wheat:** Substituting a portion of the white flour with whole wheat flour adds a nutty flavor and increased fiber, though it may result in a slightly denser crumb.
*   **Sourdough Focaccia:** For those who love the tangy complexity of sourdough, replace the commercial yeast with an active sourdough starter. This will require longer fermentation times but yields incredible flavor.

Focaccia Genovese is more than just a recipe; it's an experience. From the satisfying process of kneading the dough to the intoxicating aroma filling your kitchen, and finally, to the sheer delight of biting into that perfectly crisp yet tender slice, it embodies the heart and soul of Italian baking. Give this recipe a try, and you'll understand why this simple flatbread has captivated hearts for centuries.
